about.description:
Currently a cybersecurity student, I'm passionate about the world of IT, and more specifically about protecting networks and systems. Naturally curious and always looking for new challenges, I chose to build my path in this fast-evolving field.

It was throughout my studies that cybersecurity became an obvious calling: in an increasingly connected world, protecting systems and data has become a major challenge. I want to contribute to this mission by building solutions that are ever safer, more efficient, and adapted to today's threats.

I love learning, experimenting, and building, whether through personal projects or in a professional setting, and I enjoy putting my skills into practice to keep growing.

If you'd like to know more about me or get in touch, feel free to explore this site... or download my CV!

expertise.title: Networks & Infrastructure
expertise.description: Designing and securing corporate network architectures: VLAN/DMZ segmentation, IPsec VPNs, Cisco ASA firewalls, and centralized monitoring (Centreon).

expertise.title: Defensive Security & SOC
expertise.description: Deploying containerized SOC stacks (Wazuh SIEM, n8n SOAR) and automating real-time incident detection and response.

expertise.title: Cyber Threat Intelligence
expertise.description: Analyzing and contextualizing threats (TTPs, IOCs) using the MITRE ATT&CK framework and OpenCTI, as part of my apprenticeship at Sopra Steria.

projects.main_description: Discover my technical achievements and innovations

list.title: Secure Multi-Site Network Infrastructure
list.description: Design of a secure multi-site network infrastructure simulating a production environment, with DMZ segmentation, encrypted VPN tunnels, and centralized monitoring via Centreon.
list.long_description: Implementation of a multi-site corporate network infrastructure simulating a secure production architecture. Deployment of an interconnected dual role: a managed IT services provider and its SME client, integrating zone segmentation (DMZ), inter-VLAN routing, encrypted tunnel interconnection, and centralized monitoring.
skills_acquired: Configuring Site-to-Site IPsec and Client-to-Site OpenVPN tunnels
Securing Wi-Fi with WPA2-Enterprise (RADIUS / local WLC)
Setting up a reverse proxy and tiered DMZs

list.title: Responding to a Cyber-Attack
list.description: Design of a complete corporate SOC (SIEM, CTI, SOAR) able to detect cyberattacks in real time and respond automatically on the firewall, with no human intervention.
list.long_description: Full simulation of the three pillars of a corporate SOC (SIEM, CTI, and SOAR). Design of a virtualized infrastructure able to detect cyberattacks in real time and carry out automated remediation actions on the firewall, with no human intervention.
skills_acquired: Deploying and architecting a containerized SOC stack
Managing and qualifying Threat Intelligence feeds (IoC Abuse.ch / ANSSI)
Technical curiosity, responsiveness, and analytical thinking

list.title: Threat Landscape Report (EDM)
list.description: Threat intelligence report on Kali365, a Phishing-as-a-Service platform targeting Microsoft 365, produced during my apprenticeship on the Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) team at Sopra Steria.
list.long_description:
Production of a Threat Landscape Report (EDM) within Sopra Steria's Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) team: an intelligence deliverable on a current cyber threat, meant to give SOC teams an actionable base for Threat Hunting.

Topic chosen: Kali365, a Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform targeting Microsoft 365 environments, which was the subject of an FBI/IC3 alert in May 2026. The threat relies on abusing the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant flow (Device Code Flow): the victim approves, on a legitimate Microsoft page, a code generated by the attacker, who then obtains OAuth access tokens without needing the password or a new MFA challenge.

The work covered the full CTI process: gathering and cross-referencing sources (FBI/IC3, Arctic Wolf, Palo Alto Unit 42, ANY.RUN, Guardz, Todyl), technical analysis of the attack chain (initial phishing, collection via Microsoft Graph, persistence through tokens, defense evasion via mailbox rules), mapping to MITRE ATT&CK (T1566 Phishing, T1078 Valid Accounts, T1114 Email Collection, T1534 Internal Spearphishing), IOC extraction and formulation of detection hypotheses (Device Code Flow anomalies, geographic/ASN inconsistencies, Impossible Travel, mass mailbox access), before capitalizing the findings in OpenCTI.

Delivered as a summary report and a presentation, with the level of technical detail adapted to the audience (technical analysts vs. non-technical stakeholders).
skills_acquired: Methodology for producing a Threat Landscape Report (EDM)
Formulating detection hypotheses from cloud logs (Entra ID)
Simplifying complex technical concepts (OAuth, tokens, Device Code Flow)

timeline.title: MSc in Cybersecurity
timeline.description: Work-study Master of Science aiming for in-depth technical expertise in security (pentesting, SOC, DevSecOps, cloud) and governance (GRC). Preparation for professional certifications and a Level 7 RNCP Title.

timeline.title: BUT in Networks & Telecommunications

timeline.title: General Baccalaureate

timeline.description: Obtained the baccalaureate with a strong foundation in sciences.

hobbies.description: I have been playing the guitar since 2018. I enjoy playing it regularly, especially with my friends and for my family during the holidays!

hobbies.description: One day, I would like to travel the world; other countries and their cultures fascinate me. Not to forget the culinary culture!

list.title: CTI Analyst (Apprenticeship)
list.summary: Apprenticeship as a Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) analyst. Contributing to system protection through threat analysis and contextualization.

tasks: Identifying TTPs (Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures) and IOCs (Indicators of Compromise)

list.title: Internship
list.summary: This internship allowed me to consolidate my skills in networking, systems, cybersecurity, and technical support. It also taught me the importance of rigor, documentation, and clear communication with users from various backgrounds.

tasks: Migration of a Cisco firewall to SonicWall
Installation of UniFi Wi-Fi access points
Development of a PowerShell script for automated extraction of user information
Deployment of Poly CCX 400 phones (Teams compatible)
Creation of an illustrated procedure to standardize installations
Analysis of fraudulent websites and prevention
Implementation of a secure guest Wi-Fi with a captive portal
